message:
chore(beads): record batched-operand vmap gather fast-path task (frankenjax-5qbt)

Add frankenjax-5qbt (Fast-path batched-operand vmap gather, priority
2, type task, status in_progress) capturing the next BatchTrace
gather optimization slice.

Issue context:
BatchTrace gather currently direct-runs the unbatched-operand /
batched-indices path, but batched-operand cases (where the operand
itself has a batch axis, with either shared or batched indices)
still fall through to the generic per-row slice/eval/stack
fallback. That fallback path has measurable overhead vs a flattened
operand/index direct dispatch, especially for typical embedding-style
gather workloads where the operand is a [batch, vocab, hidden]
tensor and indices are either [batch, seq] (batched) or [seq]
(shared).

Acceptance per the issue body:
- Add a flattened operand/index direct path covering batched operand
  with shared indices and batched operand with batched indices.
- Preserve gather axis-0 semantics and shape results — no behavioral
  change visible to callers, only a faster path.
- Focused tests covering both shared-index and batched-index
  variants, plus the non-axis-0 fall-through case to confirm the
  generic path still runs when the new fast path is not applicable.
- Criterion before/after evidence on the gather batching benchmark
  (matching the bd-os0um/bd-6ll5a pattern).
- Workspace gates: cargo test/clippy/fmt + rch.

Beads-only commit; the implementation lands in subsequent commits
on crates/fj-dispatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
